sect1.title: Reporting Codes for Payment
sect1.para: Example 1500 Claim Form
ABC Health Plan
123 Money St
Anywhere, AK 00000
HEALTH INSURANCE CLAIM FORM APPROVED BY NATIONAL UNIFORM CLAIM COMMITTEE (NUCC) 02/12
PICA

listitem.para: MEDICARE MEDICAID TRICARE CHAMPVA GROUP FECA OTHER 1a. INSURED'S I.D. NUMBER (For Program in Item 1)

sect1.para: CHAMPUS (Medicaid #) (Sponsor's SSN) (VA File #) HEALTH PLAN BLK LUNG 123456789 (Medicare #) (ID#) (SSN or ID) (SSN) (SSN or ID) (SSN) (ID)

listitem.para: PATIENT'S NAME (Last Name, First Name, Middle Initial) 3. PATIENT'S BIRTH DATE 4. INSURED'S NAME (Last Name, First Name, Middle Initial)
Smith, Patient MM DD YY SEX Smith, Parent
PATIENT'S ADDRESS (No., Street) 6. PATIENT RELATIONSHIP TO INSURED 7. INSURED'S ADDRESS (No., Street)
123 Main St Self Spouse Child Other
PATIENT STATUS CITY STATE
Single Married Other Anywhere KS
OTHER INSURED'S NAME (Last Name, First Name, Middle Initial) ZIP CODE TELEPHONE (Include Area Code)
IS PATIENT'S CONDITION RELATED TO: 11. INSURED'S POLICY GROUP OR FECA NUMBER
Smith, Parent 9876543210
EMPLOYMENT? (Current or Previous) 12. PATIENT'S OR AUTHORIZED PERSON'S SIGNATURE I authorize the release of any medical or other information necessary

sect1.para: YES NO to process this claim. I also request payment of government benefits either to myself or to the party who accepts assignment below.

listitem.para: AUTO ACCIDENT? PLACE (State)
YES NO Signature on file DATE
OTHER ACCIDENT?

sect1.para: YES NO 13. INSURED'S OR AUTHORIZED PERSON'S SIGNATURE I authorize payment of medical benefits to the undersigned physician or supplier for

listitem.para: INSURANCE PLAN NAME OR PROGRAM NAME services described below.
Another Good Plan

sect1.para: Signature on file READ BACK OF FORM BEFORE COMPLETING & SIGNING THIS FORM.
10d. RESERVED FOR NUCC USE 14. DATE OF CURRENT ILLNESS, INJURY, or PREGNANCY (LMP) 15. OTHER DATE

listitem.para: MM DD YY MM DD YY
DATES PATIENT UNABLE TO WORK IN CURRENT OCCUPATION
FROM TO
MM DD YY MM DD YY
NAME OF REFERRING PROVIDER OR OTHER SOURCE 17a. 18. HOSPITALIZATION DATES RELATED TO CURRENT SERVICES
I.D. NUMBER OF REFERRING PROVIDER FROM TO
MM DD YY MM DD YY
ADDITIONAL CLAIM INFORMATION (Designated by NUCC) 20. OUTSIDE LAB? $ CHARGES
YES NO
DIAGNOSIS OR NATURE OF ILLNESS OR INJURY (Relate A-L to service line below) (24E) 22. RESUBMISSION
Z00.129 B. Z29.11 C. Z23 CODE ORIGINAL REF. NO.
PRIOR AUTHORIZATION NUMBER
A. DATE(S) OF SERVICE B. C. D. PROCEDURES, SERVICES, OR SUPPLIES E. F. G. H. I. J.

sect1.para: FROM TO PLACE MODIFIER (Explain Unusual Circumstances) DIAGNOSIS DAYS EPSDT ID. RENDERING MM DD YY MM DD YY OF CPT/HCPCS MODIFIER POINTER OR Family QUAL. PROVIDER ID. #
5 NPI
6 NPI

listitem.para: FEDERAL TAX I.D. NUMBER SSN EIN 26. PATIENT'S ACCOUNT NO. 27. ACCEPT ASSIGNMENT? 28. TOTAL CHARGE 29. AMOUNT PAID 30. Rsvd for NUCC Use
SIGNATURE OF PHYSICIAN OR SUPPLIER 32. SERVICE FACILITY LOCATION INFORMATION 33. BILLING PROVIDER INFO & PH #

sect1.para: INCLUDING DEGREES OR CREDENTIALS Pediatric Practice (I certify that the statements on the reverse 111 Some St

listitem.para: apply to this bill and are made a part thereof.) Anywhere, US 99999
Signature on file Pediatric Practice NPI 01234567890
111 Some St
DATE Anywhere, US 99999

sect1.para: 02/23/2026 NUCC Instruction Manual available at: www.nucc.org PLEASE PRINT OR TYPE

Documentation of diagnoses, signs and symptoms, or other reasons for a service is not accomplished through code assignment. Per official guidance from Coding Clinic, selection of a code cannot replace a written diagnostic statement. Multiple clinical conditions may be represented by a single diagnosis code, and more than one code may be required to fully describe a condition. Codes should be assigned to the documented diagnostic statement.

listitem.para: When codes are selected by physicians at the time of EHR documentation, it is advised to have trained administrative staff verify the levels of service, need for modifiers, and compliance with ICD-10-CM, CPT, and payer policies. EHRs fail to show complete code descriptors and/or ICD-10-CM instructions (eg, exclusion notes).

For practices that use a printed encounter form, including 50 to 100 of the most used diagnoses and their respective codes on the encounter form allows the physician or QHP to mark the appropriate code(s), indicating which is primary.
If a specific diagnosis code is not included on the form, write it in.

sect1.para: ✖ Do not select a diagnosis code that is "closest to" the diagnosis.
Pediatric Office Superbill 2026 is a convenient card that includes the most-reported CPT and ICD-10-CM codes (www.aap.org/Pediatric-Office-Superbill-2026).

sect4.title: Reporting Codes for Payment
sect4.para: In addition to standard code sets, HIPAA has established electronic transaction standards for patient benefit inquiries and submission of health claims and related reports.

listitem.para: Claims for professional services are typically submitted electronically in a format known as 837P (professional claim) as currently configured in version 5010 (ASC X12N).

Paper claim form submissions using the 1500 claim form, version 02/12, are allowed for providers who meet an exception to the HIPAA requirements for electronic claim submission (eg, provider with <10 full-time employees).

Paper claim submissions increase the length of time between submission and payment and the chance of error in either form completion or claims processing. It is advisable to submit electronic claims whenever possible.

The 1500 claim form contains the same information as an electronic claim and is valuable for demonstrating how codes and other information necessary for claims processing must berelayed to payers. An example of a completed 1500 claim form is found in Figure 1-1.

sect4.title: Linking the Diagnosis to the Service

listitem.para: Every claim line for a physician service must be linked to the appropriate ICD-10-CM code that represents the reason for the service.
Each ICD-10-CM code is placed into a diagnosis field labeled "21A–L."

A diagnosis pointer in field "24E" indicates which diagnosis codes relate to each service line by indicating the letter(s) of the related diagnosis field. Up to 4 diagnosis fields may be linked to a service line.

The first-listed code on the claim should be the principal reason for the service unless the tabular instructions direct the user to "code first" another reason (eg, code first routine child health examination [Z00.121, Z00.129] when immunizations [Z23] are provided at the time of a preventive E/M service).

When using an EHR, physicians should list the primary diagnosis first in the EHR and make certain that the software does not reorder the codes alphabetically or numerically. In addition, the EHR should beable to link diagnosis codes to the appropriate services.
The diagnosis code may be the same or different for each service performed.

sect4.para: ▶ When a child is diagnosed with a UTI, the ICD-10-CM code for UTI (N39.0) is linked to the E/M service (eg, 99213) and to an in-office dipstick urinalysis performed without microscopy (81002).

sect4.title: Other Coding and Billing Information
sect4.para: Correct code assignment and claim completion are 2 important steps in compliant and effective practice management. But many other processes and policies are required. Refer to Chapter 4 for additional guidance on coding and billing procedures.

listitem.para: indicates a new code; ▲, revised; †, re-sequenced; ✚, add-on; ✱, modifier51 exempt; and ♦, synchronous interactive audio.

sect4.para: To test your knowledge of the information presented in this chapter, complete the quiz found at the end of it, after the resources. Answers to each quiz are found in Appendix II. Add to your knowledge through the information provided in other chapters that discuss codes for specific types of service.
Readers of this chapter should generally know the code sets used to report professional services. Following are takeaways from this chapter:

listitem.para: The 4 code sets that are used for reporting professional services are ICD-10-CM, HCPCS, CPT, and the NDC.

To correctly select codes in ICD-10-CM, it is important to recognize and follow instructions found in the alphabetical index and tabular list to locate the most specific code for documented diagnoses.

HCPCS procedure codes may be reported when no CPT code describes the service provided or when the narrative (descriptor) differs from and more accurately describes the service provided than a similar CPT code.
Use of CPT guidelines and full code descriptors is important incorrect coding. Correct coding is critical to obtain correct claims payment.
Every claim line for a physician service must be linked to the appropriate ICD-10-CM code that represents the reason for the service.

sect4.title: AAP Coding Assistance and Education
sect4.para: AAP Coding and Payment Hassle Hotline Form (www.aap.org/en/practice-management/child-health-finance-payment-strategy/coding-payment-hotline)

AAP ICD-10-CM resources (www.aap.org/en/practice-management/child-health-finance-payment-strategy/coding-and-valuation/icd-10-cm-resources) Pediatric Coding Q&A: Expert Advice From the AAP Coding Hotline (www.aap.org/en/catalog/categories/coding/pediatric-coding-qa-expert-advice-from-the-aap-coding-hotline-paperback)
Pediatric ICD-10-CM 2026: A Manual for Provider-Based Coding (www.aap.org/Pediatric-ICD-10-CM-2026-11th-Edition-Paperback)

sect4.title: AAP Pediatric Coding Newsletter
sect4.para: "Back to Basics: Reporting National Drug Codes," September 2023 (https://doi.org/10.1542/pcco book233 document003) "Coding Review: Preparticipation Physical Evaluations," July 2024 (https://doi.org/10.1542/pcco book243 document003)
"CPT 2024: Reporting Unlisted Procedure Codes," December 2023 (https://doi.org/10.1542/pcco book236 document004)

ICD-10-CM article collection (https://publications.aap.org/codingnews/collection/510/ICD-10-CM) "Use Diagnosis Coding Conventions to Avoid Denied Claims," June 2024 (https://doi.org/10.1542/pcco book242 document003)
"Understanding Age Ranges in Current Procedural Terminology," May 2023 (https://doi.org/10.1542/pcco book229 document005)

sect4.title: Current Procedural Terminology
sect4.para: Category I immunization codes (www.ama-assn.org/practice-management/cpt/category-i-immunization-codes) Category II code descriptors and clinical topics listing (www.ama-assn.org/practice-management/cpt/category-ii-codes)

Category III codes (www.ama-assn.org/practice-management/cpt/category-iii-codes) ● indicates a new code; ▲, revised; #, re-sequenced; ✚, add-on; ✱, audiovisual technology; and ♦, synchronous interactive audio
